assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows NO (not affected) for all supported versions — every version ships a dependency version outside the CVE's affected range
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Version Impact Table shows all 7 versions (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4) with Affected? = **NO**. Each ships serde_json 1.0.137-1.0.139, all above the fixed version threshold of 1.0.135. The file explicitly states: 'No stream ships a vulnerable version of serde_json.'

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing as Not a Bug with resolution, not creating remediation tasks (Step 7 Case C)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md states 'Decision: Close as Not a Bug' and 'Case C applies'. Section 4 proposes 'Transition TC-8002 to Closed with resolution Not a Bug'. Section 'Step 7 Remediation Tasks' states: 'No remediation tasks are needed. No versions are affected, so Case C (close as Not a Bug) applies. No upstream backport or downstream propagation tasks are created.'

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting the version impact evidence — listing each version and its dependency version as proof of non-impact
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md Section '2. Add Triage Comment' includes a proposed Jira comment with a table listing each 2.2.x version (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) alongside its serde_json version (1.0.138 or 1.0.139) and Affected? = NO, concluding 'All supported versions ship serde_json >= 1.0.138, which is above the fixed version (1.0.135). The vulnerability does not apply to any shipped version. Closing as Not a Bug (not affected).'

assertion_results.text: VEX Justification is set to 'Component not Present' or appropriate value when the VEX field is configured (Step 7 VEX Justification)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md Section '3. Set VEX Justification' states: 'Set customfield_12345 (VEX Justification) to "Component not Present".' The VEX field customfield_12345 was identified in data-extraction.md. Rationale given: 'The vulnerable version of serde_json (< 1.0.135) is not present in any supported product version.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT create any remediation tasks — no upstream backport, no downstream propagation (Step 7 Case C closes the issue)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md line 65: 'No remediation tasks are needed. No versions are affected, so Case C (close as Not a Bug) applies. No upstream backport or downstream propagation tasks are created.' No remediation task creation appears anywhere in any of the three output files. The only proposed Jira actions are: affects versions note, triage comment, VEX justification, close transition, assign, and label.
